01::FR-01.01.title:
The landing kanban board: every task across all projects as cards grouped by state (active / idle / done / failed), newest first, with a sortable List view. A sidebar button creates a new Pipeline, Iterate, or Task. Each card has a state icon, actions menu, and launch button; clicking opens its detail. A multi-phase Run shows its child steps inline with a progress bar.
**Updates:** Gained: priority/domain/blocked-by badges, a project colour pill, Move-to-Backlog and Re-open, the single-session Run card and its design-gate panel, and every launch state made visible/recoverable.

01::FR-01.10.title:
Launch, resume, or relaunch a task. Clicking Launch / Resume / Relaunch runs the pre-built command straight inside the task's embedded terminal once it is ready, so the user no longer has to copy and paste it (they still can). A launch is either a freeform action or a specific pipeline phase; the app refuses to mix the two, and rejects a phase launch whose session identity does not match.
**Updates:** Gained one-click auto-execution into the embedded terminal (copying is no longer required), plus Start-Campaign and every launch state made visible and recoverable.

01::FR-01.02.title:
The task detail screen: a three-pane workspace with the project's file tree on the left, the session's conversation (readable chat with code and cleaned-up tool output) in the centre, and a file viewer on the right for Markdown, code, text, images, video, and diagrams. Pane sizes are remembered. The header has an editable title (renames apply on the next launch) and a state-aware action button. There is deliberately no chat box for typing to Claude.
**Updates:** Gained faster handling of very long transcripts and a Transcript/Terminal toggle; later reworked into the Files and Terminal three-card layout.

01::FR-01.35.title:
In the file viewer, Markdown files gain an Edit button that opens a rich-text editor; changes save back into the file - the first place the app writes into project files. It is limited to Markdown, size-capped, and uses a safety check so it never overwrites a file a Claude session changed underneath you: on a conflict it keeps your edits and offers to reload. It warns before saving files with tricky content (tables, raw HTML, footnotes).
**Updates:** Gained a formatting toolbar.

01::FR-01.66.title:
The Mission tab is a live, plain-language view of what a session is doing, read straight from its transcript rather than only a finished-run record - so even an ad-hoc session in progress gets a summary. Three panels: left shows a business-language summary, where things stand (Spec / Build / Test / Finalize), and clickable artifact links; the middle narrates what is happening now; the right opens artifacts on click. It reports only what the transcript actually contains - no invented activity - and never writes anything.
**Updates:** Gained a plain-language event narrator, the Record rail plus Operation card and three-card layout, and six lifecycle stages with campaign-progress awareness - then rebuilt to read the live transcript, with context-aware artifacts per scenario and a stage derived from the session's real phase rather than coarse tool signals.

01::FR-01.28.title:
A real terminal embedded in the task detail screen (a Transcript/Terminal toggle) that connects to a live shell for that task. Only safe shell programs may run - it will never start Claude itself. The first viewer can type; extra viewers watch read-only. The shell is closed when the last viewer leaves and forced closed after 30 idle minutes. Scrollback is saved and replayed when you reconnect.
**Updates:** Gained keyboard copy/paste over remote access, Claude's own clipboard relay, mobile touch-scrolling, scrollback replay across reconnects, and a light/dark theme matching Claude Code.

01::FR-01.29.title:
Paste an image straight into the embedded terminal: if the clipboard holds a picture it is uploaded, validated, size-capped (keeping only the most recent few), and its file path is typed into the shell; otherwise pasted text is sent as text. If the project's ignore file is missing the pastes folder, a toast offers a one-click way to add it.
**Updates:** Gained multi-line paste handling so a pasted multi-line prompt no longer submits on its first line.

01::FR-01.04.title:
One cross-project Inbox that gathers every open question a running session is waiting on (for example a multiple-choice prompt) across all your tracked tasks. Each row links back to the task it came from, and a menu-style question can be dismissed to silence the reminder.
**Updates:** Gained detection of plain-text 'how should I proceed?' questions (which clear once you reply), and an honest note that they are answered in the task's terminal, not the Inbox.

01::FR-01.03.title: Add, edit, and remove projects in the Command Center. A 4-step setup wizard collects the project's name, folder path, technology profile (auto-suggested), and a colour tag. 01::FR-01.59.title:
The Projects page rebuilt from a plain table into a gallery of project cards - colour dot, name, grade pill, path, summary body, and a footer (settings, delete, task count, Open board). The body is always honest: a graded project shows a runs sparkline with run/requirement/score counts and a quote from its latest run; an ungraded one says 'no runs yet - grade or adopt it'; a non-Shipwright project shows its session count. Graded projects sort first, and every link goes to a page that exists.
**Updates:** Gained an honest 'N sessions' state for non-Shipwright projects; the richer project-home concept (drawer, logbook entries, scoped prompt box) was folded in.

01::FR-01.16.title:
Assembles the list of available actions for a project by layering the project's own custom actions over the built-in defaults. This list feeds the 'new task/iterate/pipeline' dialogs and the launch-button menus.
**Updates:** Gained five task-routing fields in the create dialogs (domain, priority, complexity hint, tags, and blocked-by), which projects can opt into via their own actions file.

01::FR-01.27.title:
On the Settings page, pick a registered project and see whether its action set is custom, built-in, or malformed. You can upload a replacement actions file (validated before it is saved) or reset back to the built-in defaults. Uploads are size-capped and must be valid JSON.
**Updates:** Gained the same upload directly inside the project edit dialog (plus an upload-route fix).

01::FR-01.37.title: A custom action can name a slash command (for example '/content-orchestrator') so that the task's description is combined with that command into a single prompt sent to Claude. If a custom action needs this but names no valid slash command, the app reports a clear error up front rather than failing silently or breaking at launch.

01::FR-01.45.title:
For a single-session run, when Claude reaches the design gate it produces mockups plus a review viewer and pauses for a human decision. The Command Center hosts that viewer at full fidelity and captures your per-round feedback into the project - no manual export. Clicking Resume hands the feedback back so the run revises and continues. The app writes only the temporary feedback file, never the run's own control files.
**Updates:** Gained a record view with a mockup gallery and an Approve / Request-changes decision bar.

01::FR-01.33.title:
A read-only Campaigns lane above the board that lists your Shipwright iterate campaigns. Each card shows the campaign name, a one-line intent, a done/total progress bar, and its ordered steps (complete / next / pending), plus a one-click Copy-launch button for the next step. The lane refreshes every few seconds and hides itself when every campaign is finished or none exist.
**Updates:** Gained one-click copy-launch of the next campaign step (and the concept of autonomous campaign launching).

01::FR-01.17.title: An optional Preview button that starts a project's own development web server so the user can see the app running. It only appears when the project's technology profile is a frontend with a defined dev-server command and the user hasn't switched it off; the server is shut down cleanly when the Command Center stops.

01::FR-01.43.title: A per-project compliance Control-Grade shown read-only from the project's own generated report: a colour-coded grade pill (A green, B amber, C or lower red) with the score, one-line verdict, and date, appearing on the Projects list and the board header. Clicking it opens a panel with the verdict and security details. If a project has no report, no badge is shown - nothing is invented.

01::FR-01.47.title: Turns the recorded project event log into a per-run summary (used by Mission Control, the Ship's-Log gallery, and the board) plus a grade-over-time trend. It is strictly honest: a run with no matching completion record shows nothing rather than guessing, durations are never estimated (they show 'n/a' when unknown), and derived pass/fail lamps are never presented as an authoritative verdict.

01::FR-01.51.title:
A friendly three-door front entrance before the expert setup wizard. New walks you through plain questions and shows a plan of the pipeline phases with a live 'because you said X, we'll do Y' rail. Adopt points at a folder and shows exactly what onboarding would write. Grade points at a folder or GitHub URL and shows a Control-Grade card, honestly marking anything it cannot score as 'n/a' rather than inventing a number. A readiness check gates all three doors - if a prerequisite is missing the doors are disabled and the fix named.
**Updates:** Gained the real create-then-launch wiring for the New and Adopt doors and the real read-only Grade result.

01::FR-01.38.title:
The Command Center becomes usable on tablets without changing the desktop layout at all: the sidebar collapses to icons, the board columns turn into a swipe carousel, less-important columns are hidden, and the task detail shows one pane at a time behind Files/Session/Viewer tabs - while never tearing down the live terminal when you switch.
**Updates:** Gained full phone (small-screen) support and broad mobile/tablet polish across the header, list, projects, sidebar, and board.

01::FR-01.48.title: The 'Weather Deck' visual foundation the whole app is styled on: warm neutral colours, a deep-teal accent, frosted-glass panels, a beige reading surface, and one signature photo backdrop on every screen. Its main promise is legibility - text stays readable on the photo, on glass, or on a white card - proven by an automatic contrast check that fails the build if any text/background pair drops below accessibility limits. Appearance-only; stores no data.

Empty screens (Inbox, Board, Projects) teach with one sentence and exactly one action instead of blank space, and the create dialog's phase chips show a plain-language tooltip. All this copy is static - it never fakes live data.

01::FR-01.64.title: A coherent motion system with one firm promise: for users who have animations turned off (the primary everyday case), every screen shows its complete final state instantly - all content present, counters at their final number, lists fully shown. Content is never hidden then revealed by an animation. Only meaningful moments animate (proof lines, a slide-in panel, a grade ring counting up, log entries); decorative motion is excluded, the terminal is never animated, and motion never fakes activity over data that isn't really there.

01::FR-01.65.title: The power-user layer: a command palette (Ctrl/Cmd+K) with fuzzy search over one shared list of commands, a consistent keyboard model, a comfortable/compact density toggle, a terminal focus mode, and instant cache-first screens. Crucially, all global shortcuts go inert while you type in the terminal or a text field - even the palette's own shortcut - so every keystroke still reaches the terminal unchanged. A '?' cheat-sheet lists every shortcut in both Windows and Mac form, and everything keyboard-driven also has a clickable equivalent.

01::FR-01.05.title: A read-only diagnostics page showing the detected Claude CLI version, the folder the app is reading technology profiles from, sample launch commands for each shell, and the sessions currently being observed. A warning banner appears when the installed Claude CLI is older than the supported minimum.

01::FR-01.06.title: A minimal settings page. Most settings now live inside the user's own Claude client (model, autonomy, plugins); the Command Center keeps only its own display preferences, saved safely so two tabs can't corrupt them.

01::FR-01.31.title: By default the app's servers are reachable only from the same machine, for safety. Reaching them from other devices on your LAN or over Tailscale is opt-in: one network-profile setting flips both halves of the app to a matching, coherent access mode at once.

01::FR-01.49.title: One installer command that both installs and updates the whole system (the Shipwright plugins and the Command Center), first run and every run after. It checks prerequisites up front and refuses loudly if a required tool (Claude, Python, Node, git) is missing, rather than leaving a broken install. It never starts a Claude session; if the app is already running it attaches to a same-or-newer one, safely swaps an older one, and leaves an unrelated program on the port untouched.
